diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dysuria is a cardinal symptom of lower UTI; with frequency it markedly raises the probability of cystitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinary frequency, urgency and incomplete emptying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Detrusor irritability from bladder-wall inflammation produces frequency, urgency and a sense of incomplete voiding.
Inflammatory haematuria is common in florid cystitis when other infective features are present.
Visible haematuria is a cancer red flag in general, but is low-probability here given the concurrent florid infection; mandates confirmation of resolution after treatment.
Turbid, malodorous urine reflects pyuria and bacteriuria; supportive but non-specific.
Suprapubic discomfort localises the process to the bladder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent, post-coital, culture-proven UTI pattern (four in twelve months)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent, post-coital, culture-proven pattern with inter-episode resolution defines recurrent uncomplicated UTI and drives the prevention/imaging plan.
Fever/rigors are hallmark of ascending/systemic infection; their absence keeps the process in the lower tract.
Negative CVA tenderness argues against upper-tract involvement — but this is a clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot perform, so it is sealed.
Mild-to-moderate suprapubic tenderness supports bladder inflammation; a clinician palpation finding sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urine dipstick positive for nitrites and leukocyte esterase (and blood)

investigation_details.expected_result: Positive nitrites, positive leukocyte esterase, positive blood
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without urinalysis the infection cannot be confirmed and antibiotic choice/appropriateness cannot be judged.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive nitrites plus leukocyte esterase substantially raises the probability of bacterial UTI; a clinician/point-of-care test the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Midstream urine culture identifying a uropathogen with sensitivities

investigation_details.expected_result: Growth of a single uropathogen (commonly E. coli) with an antibiogram
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Empirical therapy may not cover a resistant organism; recurrence work-up loses its microbiological anchor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive MSU culture confirms the causative organism and guides targeted therapy; a laboratory result never available in real time via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal — no obstruction, stone or mass
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: For recurrent UTI with visible haematuria, omitting imaging risks missing a structural contributor or an underlying lesion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal ultrasound reduces the likelihood of stone/obstruction/mass; an imaging step the AI cannot perform.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dysuria + frequency/urgency + a positive nitrite/leukocyte dipstick together strongly confirm a lower UTI flare.

A negative fever/rigors and flank-tenderness screen keeps the infection lower-tract and justifies an urgent in-person (not emergency) pathway — while these same features define the escalation trigger.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line confirmation of infection at the same-day in-person assessment the AI arranges.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Midstream urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies the uropathogen and its sensitivities to guide/adjust targeted therapy; essential in a recurrent pattern.
Indicated for recurrent UTI with visible haematuria to exclude a structural contributor; arranged on follow-up, not tonight.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat urinalysis after treatment to confirm haematuria resolution
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Visible haematuria must be documented to clear once the infection is treated; persistence triggers a formal haematuria work-up.
Not required for a systemically-well, afebrile lower-UTI flare; reserve for suspected pyelonephritis/sepsis. Ordering routinely is over-investigation.
No colicky loin-to-groin pain to suggest an obstructing stone; not indicated at this presentation.
